steps.title: Identify what the blank needs to accomplish
steps.content: Researchers face a shortcoming: their study had a “myopic focus on a few countries in North America and Western Europe, partly due to limited data availability.” Now they are “gaining new access to banking records located in nations in Africa, such as Morocco, and Eastern Europe, such as Poland.” The blank needs a verb for what researchers can now do to their original geographic-bias problem—something that reduces or addresses the limitation.

steps.title: Match the word to the action
steps.content:
Mitigate means to make something less severe or reduce its negative impact.

With comprehensive data from previously overlooked regions (Africa and Eastern Europe), researchers could reduce the geographic bias that had limited their earlier work.

The other choices don't capture this: presuppose means to assume beforehand, categorize means to classify, and validate means to confirm as correct.

That confirms C is correct.
